summary: Matrix mix captures a stellar May 1977 performance – swinging Bertha, chilling Estimated, a first‑time I Know You Rider and the rare second take of Jack‑A‑Roe.
review: Fans rave about this show as one of the highlights of May 1977, citing the electrifying Bertha, the haunting Estimated, the fresh first I Know You Rider and a uniquely different second rendition of Jack‑A‑Roe. The matrix recording is praised for its clear mix and atmospheric room echo that adds to the mystique, making it both technically solid and emotionally resonant.

ai_rating.rationale: Three high‑rating reviews (4–5 stars) praise the musical performance; one notes a good matrix recording. The combination of an excellent show and solid technical quality yields a rating in the upper‑mid range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Matrix mix balances soundboard clarity with audience ambience; listeners note pleasant room echo that enhances the vibe, and no major drop‑outs or distortion are reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows naturally, highlighted by a rare second performance of Jack‑A‑Roe and a powerful early I Know You Rider; reviewers describe the night as one of the most compelling May 1977 shows.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir is humorously interrupted by an audience shout, but no criticism of his playing is given.
